title: AMMA: A Multi-Chiplet Memory-Centric Architecture for Low-Latency 1M Context Attention Serving
summary: All current LLM serving systems place the GPU at the center, from production-level attention-FFN disaggregation to NVIDIA's Rubin GPU-LPU heterogeneous platform. Even academic PIM/PNM proposals still treat the GPU as the central hub for cross-device communication. Yet the GPU's compute-rich architecture is fundamentally mismatched with the memory-bound nature of decode-phase attention, inflating serving latency while wasting power and die area on idle compute units. The problem is compounded as reasoning and agentic workloads push context lengths toward one million tokens, making attention...

metadata.comment: Memory-centric multi-chiplet attention-serving architecture targeting the bandwidth bottleneck of million-token decode attention.

title: CoQuant: Joint Weight-Activation Subspace Projection for Mixed-Precision LLMs
summary: Post-training quantization (PTQ) has become an important technique for reducing the inference cost of Large Language Models (LLMs). While recent mixed-precision methods improve ultra-low bit quantization by preserving critical subspaces in high precision, they typically construct these subspaces relying solely on activation statistics. This ignores the fundamental nature of linear operations, where the output perturbation is jointly driven by both activation and weight quantization noise. In this paper, we propose CoQuant, a joint weight-activation subspace projection method. By theoretical...

metadata.comment: Derives a closed-form weighted PCA subspace using joint weight and activation covariances for mixed-precision LLM quantization.

title: Associative-State Universal Transformers: Sparse Retrieval Meets Structured Recurrence
summary: We study whether a structured recurrent state can serve as a compact associative backbone for language modeling while still supporting exact retrieval. We introduce UniMatrix, a Universal Transformer style family that reuses a shared recurrent block across depth and augments it with hybrid state updates, a ROSA-style residual path, and token-conditioned embedding modulation. We evaluate these models on byte-level WikiText-2, synthetic associative recall, throughput profiling on Apple MPS, and a corrected benchmark for triple-token interactions. At small scale, UniMatrix-Core and UniMatrix-R...
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

metadata.comment: Studies structured recurrent state plus sparse slot retrieval, showing explicit retrieval is needed for exact associative lookup in sequence models.
metadata.primary_topic_label: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

title: Lyapunov-Guided Self-Alignment: Test-Time Adaptation for Offline Safe Reinforcement Learning
summary: Offline reinforcement learning (RL) agents often fail when deployed, as the gap between training datasets and real environments leads to unsafe behavior. To address this, we present SAS (Self-Alignment for Safety), a transformer-based framework that enables test-time adaptation in offline safe RL without retraining. In SAS, the main mechanism is self-alignment: at test time, the pretrained agent generates several imagined trajectories and selects those satisfying the Lyapunov condition. These feasible segments are then recycled as in-context prompts, allowing the agent to realign its behavi...
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Lyapunov-guided imagined trajectories reused as in-context prompts for offline safe RL test-time adaptation.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

title: Uncertainty-Aware Predictive Safety Filters for Probabilistic Neural Network Dynamics
summary: Predictive safety filters (PSFs) leverage model predictive control to enforce constraint satisfaction during deep reinforcement learning (RL) exploration, yet their reliance on first-principles models or Gaussian processes limits scalability and broader applicability. Meanwhile, model-based RL (MBRL) methods routinely employ probabilistic ensemble (PE) neural networks to capture complex, high-dimensional dynamics from data with minimal prior knowledge. However, existing attempts to integrate PEs into PSFs lack rigorous uncertainty quantification. We introduce the Uncertainty-Aware Predictiv...
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Predictive safety filter with reachable-set uncertainty handling for probabilistic ensemble neural dynamics.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

title: Unified 4D World Action Modeling from Video Priors with Asynchronous Denoising
summary: We propose X-WAM, a Unified 4D World Model that unifies real-time robotic action execution and high-fidelity 4D world synthesis (video + 3D reconstruction) in a single framework, addressing the critical limitations of prior unified world models (e.g., UWM) that only model 2D pixel-space and fail to balance action efficiency and world modeling quality. To leverage the strong visual priors of pretrained video diffusion models, X-WAM imagines the future world by predicting multi-view RGB-D videos, and obtains spatial information efficiently through a lightweight structural adaptation: replicat...
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Unified action-conditioned 4D world model with asynchronous denoising to jointly support fast action decoding and richer future world synthesis.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

title: Momentum-Conserving Graph Neural Networks for Deformable Objects
summary: Graph neural networks (GNNs) have emerged as a versatile and efficient option for modeling the dynamic behavior of deformable materials. While GNNs generalize readily to arbitrary shapes, mesh topologies, and material parameters, existing architectures struggle to correctly predict the temporal evolution of key physical quantities such as linear and angular momentum. In this work, we propose MomentumGNN -- a novel architecture designed to accurately track momentum by construction. Unlike existing GNNs that output unconstrained nodal accelerations, our model predicts per-edge stretching and...

metadata.comment: Architecture enforces linear and angular momentum conservation by predicting edge impulses instead of unconstrained node accelerations.

title: Hyper Input Convex Neural Networks for Shape Constrained Learning and Optimal Transport
summary: We introduce Hyper Input Convex Neural Networks (HyCNNs), a novel neural network architecture designed for learning convex functions. HyCNNs combine the principles of Maxout networks with input convex neural networks (ICNNs) to create a neural network that is always convex in the input, theoretically capable of leveraging depth, and performs reliable when trained at scale compared to ICNNs. Concretely, we prove that HyCNNs require exponentially fewer parameters than ICNNs to approximate quadratic functions up to a given precision. Throughout a series of synthetic experiments, we demonstrate...

metadata.comment: Introduces a convex-by-construction architecture with stronger approximation efficiency than standard ICNNs.

title: Language Diffusion Models are Associative Memories Capable of Retrieving Unseen Data
summary: The associative-memory view of language diffusion models is a genuinely new conceptual framing with potential to open a new line of analysis.

metadata.comment: Interprets language diffusion models as associative memories and proposes conditional entropy as a probe of memorization-to-generalization transition.
metadata.spotlight_comment: The associative-memory view of language diffusion models is a genuinely new conceptual framing with potential to open a new line of analysis.

title: Exploring the Potential of Probabilistic Transformer for Time Series Modeling: A Report on the ST-PT Framework
summary: The programmable factor-graph view of transformers points toward a potentially new design paradigm beyond standard black-box sequence modeling.

metadata.comment: Recasts transformer blocks as programmable CRF inference for structured time-series modeling.
metadata.spotlight_comment: The programmable factor-graph view of transformers points toward a potentially new design paradigm beyond standard black-box sequence modeling.

title: FutureWorld: A Live Environment for Training Predictive Agents with Real-World Outcome Rewards
summary: A live real-world RL environment for predictive agents feels both broadly timely and direction-setting, not just a niche benchmark.
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Turns live future prediction into a reinforcement-learning environment with delayed real-world outcome rewards and continual updating.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning
metadata.spotlight_comment: A live real-world RL environment for predictive agents feels both broadly timely and direction-setting, not just a niche benchmark.

title: Causal Learning with Neural Assemblies
summary: The paper proposes an unusual auditable causal-learning substrate based on neural assemblies and local plasticity, which may open a distinct mechanistic direction.

metadata.comment: Shows how sparse neural assemblies with local plasticity can learn directed causal relations via an auditable mechanism.
metadata.spotlight_comment: The paper proposes an unusual auditable causal-learning substrate based on neural assemblies and local plasticity, which may open a distinct mechanistic direction.

title: Rethinking KV Cache Eviction via a Unified Information-Theoretic Objective
summary: Broadly important today: Recasts KV-cache eviction as information-capacity maximization and derives a leverage-score-based eviction rule from that objective.

metadata.comment: Recasts KV-cache eviction as information-capacity maximization and derives a leverage-score-based eviction rule from that objective.

title: Stochastic Scaling Limits and Synchronization by Noise in Deep Transformer Models
summary: Broadly important today: Continuous-time stochastic limit theory for transformers with quantitative convergence and a synchronization-by-noise result.

title: RaMP: Runtime-Aware Megakernel Polymorphism for Mixture-of-Experts
summary: Broadly important today: Routing-aware runtime dispatch for MoE kernels using a compact performance model driven by expert-histogram statistics.

title: MoRFI: Monotonic Sparse Autoencoder Feature Identification
summary: Broadly important today: Uses sparse autoencoders to identify monotonic latent directions that causally mediate hallucination and knowledge disruption during fine-tuning.

title: Lifting Embodied World Models for Planning and Control
summary: Broadly important today: Builds a lifted action-conditioned world model by composing a frozen world model with a policy from high-level waypoints to low-level controls.
